chemistry.reaction_name: photochemical synthesis of noble metal nanoparticles

substrates.substrate_A: noble metal salts (H2PtCl6, Pd(NO3)2, RhCl3, IrCl3)

chemistry.product: noble metal nanoparticles (Pt, Pd, Rh, Ir)
chemistry.scope_summary: synthesis of platinum group metal nanoparticles (Pt, Pd, Rh, Ir) with ~2.5 nm diameter using photochemical micro continuous-flow process with UV irradiation

optimization_table.key_findings: Flow rates between 87.5 and 175 μL min−1 seem to be well-suited for continuous photochemical flow synthesis of 2.5 nm platinum nanoparticles

figures.flow_path: three inlet streams converge at mixing junction, flow through photoinitiation element to produce colloidal solution
figures.reactor_geometry: tubular flow reactor with photoinitiation section
figures.key_information: Three-inlet flow synthesis system with metal salt solution, carrier medium, and PVP/HMP solution for producing noble metal nanoparticles via photoinitiation
figures.caption: Figure 1.  Microfluidic arrangement for the photochemically induced formation of metal nanoparticles in micro segmented flow.

figures.trend: bell-shaped distribution with peak around 3 nm for all flow rates
figures.caption: Figure 3.  Pd nanoparticles obtained by photochemically in- duced formation of metal nanoparticles in micro segmented flow: CPS spectra. | Figure 4.  Rh nanoparticles obtained by photochemically in- duced formation of metal nanoparticles in micro segmented flow: (a) CPS spectra, (b) DLS spectra.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: not applicable - no batch baseline reported
translation_logic.flow_advantage: controlled nucleation in segmented flow enables uniform 2.5 nm nanoparticle size with high homogeneity
translation_logic.safety_improvement: microflow process provides better control over reaction conditions

figure_descriptions: Microfluidic arrangement for photochemically induced formation of metal nanoparticles
Particle size distribution plots showing optimization of flow rates

conflicts: Flow rate units inconsistent between passes: mL/min vs μL/min in optimization figures
notes: Flow-only synthesis paper focusing on nanoparticle formation. No batch baseline for comparison. Good reactor scheme and optimization data available. Some uncertainty in exact flow rate values due to unit inconsistencies.
